bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me only moderate utility, and with no messages or concession pattern yet, I will anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (about 0.96) because it is still the opening phase at t=0.02, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has twice offered essentially the same utility to me and only changed issueB between equally valued options for me, so I do not see meaningful concession; I will only concede on the low-weight issueD, where they have consistently proposed valueC.

It is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession. The opponent’s offers are low utility for me and have not improved; they seem to prefer issueA: valueD and issueD: valueC, but I should hold firm on my high-weight issues for now and avoid repeating my exact prior bid.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession toward my strongest preferences. Their offers repeatedly use issueD:valueC, so I can concede on low-weight issueD while holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting very high utility because it is still early (t=0.08) and the opponent’s offers have not shown a consistent concession pattern toward my preferences; their latest offer is actually low utility for me. I will hold firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C while returning to my best full-utility package rather than conceding further this early.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a low-cost concession. The opponent’s offers are not improving for my utility and often place issueD at valueC or valueD, so I will hold firm on the three high-weight issues and concede issueD to valueD to avoid repeating my last bid exactly.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.12, while making a small concession on the lowest-weight issueD. The opponent’s latest offer improved substantially for me by moving issueB to valueA, but they are still not conceding on issueA or issueC, so I will hold firm on my three high-importance issues for now.

I am targeting a high utility offer around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is little reason to make major concessions on the high-weight issues. The opponent has only partially conceded, especially on issueB, but their offers remain much worse for me on issueA and issueC, so I will hold firm there while conceding the lowest-weight issueD.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a visible concession from my ideal package. The opponent has recently moved toward my preferred issueB=valueA and has offered issueA=valueB before, so I will concede issueA and the low-weight issueD while holding firm on high-value issueB and issueC.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.18, so there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s recent offers have improved on my key issues issueA and issueB, while they consistently seem willing to push issueD away from my top value, so I concede only on the low-weight issueD and hold firm on the three high-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still early at t=0.20, so only limited concession is warranted. The opponent has recently moved toward my preferred issueA and sometimes issueB values, but they continue to resist my best issueC value; with no messages to add evidence, I will hold firm on the high-weight issues and concede only on lower-weight issueD.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.88 because the negotiation is early at t=0.22, so there is no need to concede down to the opponent’s low-utility latest offer. The opponent’s offers have varied on issueA and issueB but consistently avoid my top issueC valueB, so I am making a measured concession on issueC to valueA while keeping my high-weight issueA and issueB at valueA and conceding the lower-weight issueD to valueC.

I am targeting a strong but more conciliatory utility around 0.81, since it is still early-mid negotiation and there is no need to drop close to the opponent’s low-utility offers. The opponent frequently proposes issueC: valueC and issueD: valueC/B/D, so I concede on issueC and the low-weight issueD while holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB.
